danger_signals.visual_form: Player sprite animation change or status indicator
danger_signals.meaning: Player is injured and removed from play or performance degraded

danger_signals.visual_form: Puck sprite location change to opponent player
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger of scoring against own team

danger_signals.visual_form: Opponent sprite movement toward player with puck

safety_signals.visual_form: Puck sprite attached to allied player sprite
safety_signals.meaning: Control of play, scoring opportunity available

safety_signals.visual_form: Player sprite position in offensive zone visual layout
safety_signals.meaning: Closer proximity to scoring area reduces defensive pressure

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty sprite tiles in forward direction
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passing lane or movement corridor available

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary jersey color (varies by selected NHL team)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: City name displayed (e.g., 'NEW YORK', 'LONG ISLAND' for Islanders due to NHLPA licensing restriction)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent team uses contrasting jersey color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Own team bench on one side of rink, opposing bench opposite

faction_indicators.color_identity: Contrasting jersey color to home team
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Opponent city name displayed (NHLPA license restricts team logos/NHL emblems)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: White or light jerseys typically used for away teams per NHL convention
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Positioned opposite home team bench; color-coded HUD elements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display shows equal points for both teams; neutral crowd energy implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Both teams display standard aggression and pacing in sprite movement patterns

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Game in progress - own team losing
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display shows lower number for player team
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI opponent plays with increased confidence; player team aggression may need to escalate

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Game in progress - own team winning
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score display shows higher number for player team
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponent AI increases check frequency and aggressiveness to recover

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI displays 'Playoff' mode indicator; trophy icon visible as goal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single-elimination or best-of-7 series structure affects replay logic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game over screen; final score display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Return to menu or team selection state

environmental_storytelling.example: Home team displays primary color (e.g., blue for Toronto), away team displays white/light alternate, immediately establishing faction identity
Player bench on screen-left, opponent bench on screen-right (or vice versa) reinforces territorial control and team identity
UI scoreboard displays only city names ('LONG ISLAND', 'BOSTON', 'CALGARY') rather than team nicknames, due to NHLPA-only licensing; communicates political reality of game production constraints
Puck hitting boards produces visual glass-break animation; adds physical authenticity to play-by-play action
All 24 teams from 1991-92 NHL season included with real player names and rosters; establishes comprehensive competitive legitimacy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to input commands; sprite faces direction of intended movement; can pass or shoot based on input
Sprite moves to intercept passing lanes; positions between opponent and own goal
Sprite moves directly toward player with puck; initiates body check animation when in contact range
Maintains positioning in defensive zone; sprite moves to block shooting lanes
Sprite removed from active play or animated in penalty box/sideline; team plays short-handed or substitute enters

notes: NHLPA Hockey '93 operates within licensing constraints that directly impact visual communication: absence of NHL logos and team nicknames requires city-name-only identification. This is a manifestation layer signal of the game's political/legal positioning. The game communicates world-state primarily through score display, player sprite position/animation, and jersey coloring. Danger and safety are signaled through spatial proximity of sprites and ball possession. The paradigm field was listed as 'racing' in input but should be 'sports' (ice hockey) based on actual game content.
